senses.definition: to keep following a certain regulation, belief, or agreement

examples.x: The employees must adhere to the dress code.
The company consistently adheres to ethical business practices.
Citizens are required to adhere to the laws of the country.
The students were expected to adhere to the academic integrity policy.
It is crucial to adhere to safety regulations in the laboratory.

senses.definition: to act in accordance with rules, regulations, or requests

examples.x: Employees are expected to comply with the company's code of conduct.
Drivers must comply with traffic laws to ensure road safety.
The company implemented new security measures, and everyone needs to comply.
Over the years, the organization has complied with industry standards.
Last month, the construction team complied with the revised building codes.

senses.definition: to give someone permission to do something or to agree to do it

examples.x: Patients need to consent to a surgery or medical procedure before it can be performed.
Individuals involved in a contract must consent to the terms and conditions outlined in the agreement.
She consented to the use of her artwork in the exhibition.
With a nod, he consented to her request for a raise.
The board unanimously consented to the proposed changes in the policy.

senses.definition: to refuse to respect a person of authority or to observe a law, rule, etc.

examples.x: The rebellious teenager decided to defy the instructions given by their parents.
Citizens may choose to defy unjust laws as a form of protest against the government.
The outspoken employee was willing to defy company policies in order to advocate for change.
Some activists defy social norms to challenge and reshape cultural expectations.
The activists are defying the government's attempt to suppress freedom of speech.

senses.definition: to comply with laws or regulations

examples.x: The company ensures that all employees observe workplace safety regulations.
It is important for businesses to observe tax laws and regulations to avoid penalties.
As a responsible citizen, it is crucial to observe traffic laws and regulations to promote road safety.
The school strictly observes anti-bullying policies to create a safe and inclusive learning environment for students.
The restaurant must observe food safety regulations to maintain hygiene standards and prevent foodborne illnesses.

senses.definition: persuasive in a way that captures attention or convinces effectively

examples.x: The speaker delivered a compelling argument that persuaded many to change their views.
The documentary presented compelling evidence to support its central thesis.
His artwork is visually compelling, drawing viewers in with its vibrant colors and bold shapes.
The entrepreneur's pitch was so compelling that investors were eager to fund the project.
His compelling argument changed many opinions in the room.

senses.definition: officially allowed or accepted according to the rules or laws that apply to a particular situation

examples.x: The company's operations were deemed legitimate after passing all regulatory inspections.
His credentials were legitimate and met the requirements for the job.
Only legitimate voters are allowed to participate in the election process.
The court recognized the document as legitimate evidence in the case.
The agreement was negotiated and signed under legitimate terms and conditions.

senses.definition: willing to accept, respect, and understand different behaviors, beliefs, opinions, etc.

examples.x: She has a liberal attitude towards social issues, advocating for equality and justice.
The university promotes a liberal education that encourages critical thinking and exploration of diverse viewpoints.
His liberal interpretation of the law allowed for more flexibility in its application.
The company has adopted a liberal dress code policy, allowing employees to wear casual attire.
The politician's liberal policies on healthcare and education aim to provide broader access to services for all citizens.

senses.definition: necessary as a result of a rule or law

examples.x: The completion of safety training is obligatory for all new employees before they start work.
It is obligatory for all citizens to pay their taxes by the specified deadline.
Attending the weekly team meetings is obligatory for all members.
In some cultures, it is obligatory to remove your shoes before entering someone's home.
Filling out the necessary paperwork is obligatory before starting a new job.

senses.definition: to have a moral duty or be forced to do a particular thing, often due to legal reasons
not allowed or possible

examples.x: Going on vacation this summer is out of the question due to work commitments.
When asked about a promotion, he realized it was out of the question since he lacked the necessary qualifications.
Borrowing his friend's antique car for the road trip was out of the question; it was far too valuable.
The proposal to extend the project's deadline was out of the question, as it was already behind schedule.
Considering his severe allergies, having a pet with fur was out of the question for him.

senses.definition: limited or controlled by regulations or specific conditions
(of a rule, process, etc.) strictly followed or applied

examples.x: The school has a rigorous dress code that all students must follow.
To maintain safety, the laboratory follows rigorous procedures for handling chemicals.
The university's rigorous admission process ensures that only the most qualified students are accepted.
She underwent rigorous training to prepare for the marathon.
The company follows a rigorous quality control process to ensure product reliability.
His rigorous approach to research yielded groundbreaking results in the field of medicine.
His training was rigorous, pushing him to exceed his limits.

senses.definition: serious and strict in manner or attitude, often showing disapproval or authority

examples.x: The stern teacher maintained order in the classroom with her strict rules.
Despite his stern exterior, he was fair and just in his decisions.
The stern judge delivered a harsh sentence to the defendant, emphasizing the severity of the crime.
Her stern expression conveyed her disappointment in their behavior.
She maintained a stern expression while addressing the team about their responsibilities.

senses.definition: in a manner that is less strict when punishing someone or when enforcing a law

examples.x: The judge decided to sentence the first-time offender leniently, considering their remorse.
He manages his team leniently, preferring to motivate rather than reprimand.
The company policy is applied leniently for minor infractions, focusing more on improvement than punishment.
The coach treated the player's mistake leniently, understanding it was unintentional.
The judge sentenced the first-time offender leniently, taking into account their remorse and cooperation.

senses.definition: an amount of something that is permitted

examples.x: Children receive a weekly allowance to learn the value of money and budgeting.
There is a daily allowance of one hour for recreational activities in the schedule.
The doctor prescribed a daily allowance of vitamins to improve his health.
The hotel provides a generous allowance of towels for each guest during their stay.

senses.definition: the act of putting something to work

examples.x: The application of scientific principles led to significant advancements in technology.
The application of paint to the walls transformed the room into a vibrant space.
His successful application of the new technique improved production efficiency.

senses.definition: official permission to proceed or to happen

examples.x: The pilot received clearance from air traffic control to take off.
Employees need security clearance to access classified information.
The construction project cannot begin until environmental clearance is obtained.
The customs officer granted clearance for the shipment to enter the country.
She waited anxiously for medical clearance from her doctor before resuming physical activity.

senses.definition: the action of making people obey a law or regulation

examples.x: The police department is responsible for the enforcement of traffic laws in the city.
Strict enforcement of regulations ensures workplace safety.
The enforcement of international treaties requires cooperation among nations.
The government announced tougher enforcement measures to combat illegal fishing.
Effective enforcement of copyright laws is crucial to protect intellectual property rights.

senses.definition: a formal permission to do something

examples.x: He requested leave from work to attend his sister's wedding.
She was granted medical leave to recover from surgery.
Employees are entitled to annual leave for vacation purposes.
The soldier received military leave to spend time with family before deployment.

senses.definition: approval to begin a project

examples.x: The manager gives the green light for the project to proceed.
The committee discusses whether to give the green light to the proposed changes.
They received the green light to start filming the movie last week.
The team will eagerly await the green light to begin implementing their innovative ideas.
If the budget is approved, we can expect the green light for hiring new employees.

senses.definition: something that is necessary to have or do

examples.x: Comfortable shoes are a must for anyone planning to hike the trail.
For a successful career in medicine, a strong foundation in science is a must.
Regular exercise is a must for maintaining good health.
When visiting the city, trying the local cuisine is an absolute must.
A reliable internet connection is a must for remote work.

senses.definition: the action of respecting or following the instructions of someone in authority

examples.x: The soldier's strict obedience to orders earned him a commendation.
Parents often expect obedience from their children to ensure their safety.
The dog's obedience to commands was impressive during the training session.
Obedience to the rules is essential for maintaining order in the classroom.
The monks took vows of poverty, chastity, and obedience to their abbot.

senses.definition: the action of refusing to approve, accept, consider, or support something

examples.x: The author's manuscript faced multiple rejections before finally being published.
Her application for the scholarship was met with rejection, leaving her disappointed.
The proposal for the new policy was met with rejection by the board members.
He felt discouraged after the job rejection, despite his qualifications and experience.
The artist's work was met with rejection from the gallery, but she remained determined to find another venue.

senses.definition: the power one has to influence decisions and actions

examples.x: She had the final say-so in approving the budget for the project.
Without his boss's say-so, he couldn't proceed with the changes to the plan.
The committee's say-so was necessary before any new policies could be implemented.
The manager's say-so determined which employees would receive promotions.
The coach's say-so influenced the team's training schedule and game strategy.

senses.definition: rules that determine what one should or should not do in a particular situation

examples.x: The employee should follow the dos and don'ts outlined in the company handbook.
The teacher emphasizes the dos and don'ts of classroom behavior to maintain a productive learning environment.
Remember to review the dos and don'ts of online etiquette before engaging in social media interactions.
The team carefully followed the dos and don'ts during the project to ensure its success.
The workshop will cover the dos and don'ts of effective time management.
We adhered to the dos and don'ts of camping to minimize our impact on the environment.
I will research the dos and don'ts of healthy eating to improve my diet.
If you want to succeed in this role, you must follow the dos and don'ts of professional conduct.

senses.definition: used to show compliance with a specific rule, guideline, or standard

examples.x: All employees are required to act in accordance with the company's code of ethics.
The company operates in accordance with industry regulations.
The event was organized in accordance with local laws and regulations.
All products must be manufactured in accordance with safety standards.
Students are expected to complete their assignments in accordance with the guidelines.

senses.definition: strictly adhering to established rules, procedures, or standardized practices

examples.x: The detective solved the case by the book, meticulously gathering evidence and following every procedure.
The teacher graded the exams by the book, using the established criteria and marking scheme.
The jury deliberated carefully, ensuring that their verdict was reached by the book, considering the evidence and applicable laws.
The project manager emphasized the importance of completing the project by the book, adhering to the agreed-upon timeline and deliverables.

senses.definition: to encourage someone to carry out a particular action without any reservations
to do things as one sees fit, not according to laws or rules

examples.x: Despite the company guidelines, she preferred to play by her own rules when managing her team.
As an independent artist, he decided to play by his own rules, ignoring the traditional pathways to success.
The innovative entrepreneur built his startup by playing by his own rules, defying industry norms.
In the world of high-stakes poker, he was known for playing by his own rules, often surprising his opponents.
She found success in the fashion industry by playing by her own rules and setting unique trends.

senses.definition: to be or act in accordance with a rule, standard, etc.
refusing to follow a law or rule

examples.x: The company was fined for being non-compliant with environmental regulations.
Non-compliant behavior can lead to disciplinary action in the workplace.
The patient's non-compliant attitude with medication instructions resulted in health complications.
Non-compliant vehicles were ticketed during the roadside inspection.
The landlord issued a notice to the tenant for being non-compliant with the lease agreement.

senses.definition: to officially order to send a person to prison, psychiatric hospital, etc.

examples.x: The judge decided to commit the convicted criminal to a maximum-security prison for a lengthy sentence.
After a thorough evaluation, the court determined that it was necessary to commit the individual to a psychiatric hospital for treatment.
The magistrate had to decide whether to commit the juvenile offender to a correctional facility or pursue alternative rehabilitation measures.
The mental health professional recommended committing the patient to a psychiatric ward for intensive therapy and observation.
The court ruled to commit the defendant to a rehabilitation center for substance abuse treatment instead of imprisonment.

senses.definition: supporting traditional values and beliefs and not willing to accept any contradictory change

examples.x: She was raised in a conservative household where adherence to tradition was strongly emphasized.
He held conservative views on social issues such as marriage and gender roles.
The conservative religious group opposed any changes to their traditional practices or doctrines.
They follow a conservative lifestyle rooted in tradition.
The company adopted a conservative approach to risk management.
